title: Lab 12.3: Authority Feedback
summary: The authoritative layer evaluates the edge proposal, rejects the initial move, and returns violations plus remediation guidance instead of a silent no.
decision_question: What should authoritative validation contribute besides approval or rejection?

surfaces.name: edge projection
queries: show validation feedback returned to the planner

surfaces.name: cloud authority
queries: show policy violations for the current proposal

proposal.summary: The initial proposal is preserved intact so authority can evaluate it against the full graph and trust metadata.
assumptions: edge locality still offers the desired latency gain
the routing step can be isolated from the rest of the workflow
unresolved: whether the planner can keep the route hint local while deferring the compliance-sensitive branch

validation.summary: Authority rejects the move because the original proposal attempted to decide a compliance-sensitive routing branch outside the approved trust zone.
violations: route authorization depends on policy metadata not replicated at the edge
the proposed path would weaken the audit story for sensitive tenants
guidance: keep edge planning focused on route hint generation, not final authorization
defer the compliance-sensitive branch resolution to the authoritative cloud path
authority_notes: validation transforms the proposal by explaining what must change, not just by blocking it

execution.summary: The system refuses to execute the draft because authority feedback is now part of the visible lifecycle.

trace.summary: The system now records the proposal and the compliance response, but has not yet reached an executed outcome.
queries: show the violations that blocked the first proposal
show authority guidance for the next revision

functions.planning: Planning is still local, but its output is now shaped by authority-visible constraints.
functions.validation: Validation explains why the proposal failed and what bounded change would make it acceptable.
functions.execution: Execution remains dormant until a revised proposal satisfies the authoritative checks.

expectations: Validation becomes part of discoverability when the system exposes reasons, not just results.
Authority should improve the proposal instead of remaining a black-box veto.
runtime_decisions: the system exposes why the initial move was invalid under the full capability and policy graph
the planner can now revise against explicit feedback instead of against guesswork
